news.headline: Crowd disarms knife-wielding woman at Malaysia water festival
news.summary:
A woman carrying a knife has been arrested after being tackled by the crowd during the Rain Rave Water Music Festival in Kuala Lumpur on Saturday night.
In a viral video, a man can be seen attempting to disarm the woman.

news.headline: Fuelling a continent: how China’s engineering prowess built Africa’s biggest oil refinery
news.summary:
As the energy shock unleashed by tensions in the Middle East rippled through Africa, a massive oil refinery in Nigeria’s biggest city of Lagos, owned by Africa’s wealthiest man, came to the rescue.

news.headline: Quirky China: robot plays dead for pesticide warnings; bot hugs girl, in-car toilet approved
news.summary:
A robot in a high-visibility vest carrying a loud hailer while patrolling a park in China’s northeastern Jilin province has caused much amusement online.
“We have sprayed pesticides in the park. It is highly poisonous,” the robot was exclaiming through the loud hailer.

news.headline: Trapped in a kill zone for 177 days, his wife’s voice was his lifeline
news.summary: While Roman Mongold, a Ukrainian soldier, was pinned down and surrounded by the enemy, he managed to trade voice memos with his wife thanks to a commander’s help.
